input.description: 5-minute meeting covering five topics with multiple action items per topic

output.polishedSample: Speaker 1: Hi Daniel, good to see you again. I have us scheduled for about an hour, but I've got a tight agenda, so let's see if we can move through it. I want to cover five things today: your kids' college funding, the disability insurance review, the new business succession planning, charitable giving for this year, and finally, we should talk about your wife's IRA situation. That's a lot. Let's go. Starting with college, both girls are now 13 and 15. So we're looking at college expenses starting in three years for the older one. The 529 plans we set up are growing, but at the current pace, they will be about $40,000 short for the older ones' full four years at a state school. What are my options? Three things: first, increase the monthly contribution by $200; second, we can look at converting some of the more aggressive growth allocations to a target date glide path; third, if your business has a good year, we can do a bigger lump sum contribution before December 31st. Let's do the contribution increase right away. I'll think about the lump sum after I see the third-quarter numbers. Good. I'll send you the change forms tomorrow. Now, disability insurance. The policy you have through your business owners group only covers about 40% of your income, and it has a 90-day elimination period. That doesn't sound great. It's not. For someone in your income bracket, I usually recommend supplementing with an individual policy that brings total coverage closer to 70%. The cost would be around $200 a month. Send me a couple of options. I want to compare two carriers. We'll do that. Third item is the business succession plan. You mentioned last time that your son might be interested in taking over the practice in five to seven years. Have you talked to him about it more? We had dinner about it last month. He's interested, but he wants to finish his MBA first. That's perfect timing because we have a window to set up a structured buy-sell agreement. Then start funding it now. I
output.summaryTLDR: Discussed college funding, disability insurance, business succession planning, charitable giving, and IRA planning for client's wife.
output.transcriptSample:
[00:00] Speaker 1: Hi Daniel, good to see you again.
[00:02] Speaker 1: I have us scheduled for about an hour but I've got a tight agenda so let's see if we
[00:06] Speaker 1: can move through it.
[00:07] Speaker 1: I want to cover five things today.
[00:09] Speaker 1: Your kids college funding, the disability insurance review, the new business succession
[00:14] Speaker 1: planning, charitable giving for this year.
[00:17] Speaker 1: And finally we should talk about your wife's IRA situation.
[00:20] Speaker 1: That's a lot.
[00:21] Speaker 1: Let's go.
[00:22] Speaker 1: Starting with college, both girls are now 13 and 15.
[00:26] Speaker 1: So we're looking at college expenses starting in three years for the older one.
[00:30] Speaker 1: The 529 plans we set up are growing but at the current pace will be about $40 ,000 short
[00:36] Speaker 1: for the older ones full four years at a state school.
[00:38] Speaker 1: What are my options?
[00:40] Speaker 1: Three things.
[00:41] Speaker 1: First, increase the monthly contribution by $200.
[00:44] Speaker 1: Second, we can look at converting some of the more aggressive growth allocations to a
[00:49] Speaker 1: target date glide path.
[00:50] Speaker 1: Third, if your business has a good year, we can do a bigger lump sum contribution before
[00:55] Speaker 1: December 31st. Let's do the contribution increase right away. I'll think about the lump sum after I see the third quarter numbers.
[01:03] Speaker 1: Good. I'll send you the change forms tomorrow. Now, disability insurance. The policy you have through your business owners group only covers about 40 % of your income.
[01:13] Speaker 1: And it has a 90 -day elimination period. That doesn't sound great. It's not. For someone in your income bracket I usually recommend supplementing with an individual policy that brings total coverage closer to 70%.
[01:25] Speaker 1: The cost would be around $200 a month.
[01:30] Speaker 1: Send me a couple options.
[01:32] Speaker 1: I want to compare two carriers.
[01:34] Speaker 1: We'll do.
[01:35] Speaker 1: Third item is the business succession plan.
[01:37] Speaker 1: You mentioned last time that your son might be interested in taking over the practice
[01:41] Speaker 1: in five to seven years.
[01:43] Speaker 1: Have you talked to him about it more?
[01:44] Speaker 1: We had dinner about it last month.
[01:47] Speaker 1: He's interested but he wants to finish his MBA first.
[01:50] Speaker 1: That's perfect timing because we have a window to set up a structured buy -sell agreement
[01:54] Speaker 1: Then start funding it now.
[01:56] Speaker 1: I want to introduce you to an attorney who specializes in family business transitions.
[02:00] Speaker 1: I'll set up that introduction this month.
[02:02] Speaker 1: Good.
[02:03] Speaker 1: Fourth, charitable giving.
[02:05] Speaker 1: You usually do about 25 ,000 a year split between the church and a couple of mission
[02:09] Speaker 1: organizations.
[02:10] Speaker 1: This year because of the higher business income I'd suggest we look at a donor advised fund.
[02:15] Speaker 1: You could front load three years of giving now, get the full deduction this tax year,
[02:20] Speaker 1: and distribute it over time.
[02:22] Speaker 1: How much of a tax benefit are we talking?
[02:25] Speaker 1: Roughly 12 to 15 ,000 depending on your final bracket.
[02:28] Speaker 1: I'll run the numbers and send you a one -page comparison.
[02:31] Speaker 1: Yes, do that.
[02:33] Speaker 1: And finally, your wife's IRA.
[02:35] Speaker 1: She's turning 72 next year, which means required minimum distributions kick in.
[02:40] Speaker 1: The total IRA balance is about 400 ,000 which means her first RMD will be around $15 ,000.
